Source_Species.reason: SmMAT is described as an eggplant malonyl transferase.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The eggplant source species is Solanum melongena.

Applied_Species.reason: SmMAT was used in transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The host species is Nicotiana benthamiana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The paper specifies S. melongena cv. DR2 as the eggplant material.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The text explicitly calls SmMAT a malonyl transferase.

Enzyme_Name.reason: SmMAT is explicitly referred to as malonyl transferase (SmMAT).

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: It is used in engineering eggplant and S. nigrum SGAs.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: SmMAT is in CoreEgg3, which yields malonyl-solamargine and, with SlGAME25/SlS5aR2, malonyl-soladulcine A, fitting a malonylated derivative branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Malonylation is shown as a terminal decoration in the engineered pathway.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper's combinatorial data support malonylated products, but do not directly isolate SmMAT activity on purified α-solamargine or soladulcine A within this study.

Primary_Product.reason: Modules containing SmMAT produced malonyl-solamargine and malonyl-soladulcine A.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper supports that SmMAT provides the malonyl transferase activity responsible for malonyl-solamargine and malonyl-soladulcine A formation in the reconstructed modules.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Malonyl-solamargine and malonyl-soladulcine A are the end products associated with SmMAT-containing modules.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are SGAs/alkaloids.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The Results explicitly show SmMAT-containing modules enabled production of malonylated eggplant and S. nigrum-type SGAs in N. benthamiana.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are consistent with the Results section.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: SmMAT is the only malonyl transferase included in the productive modules, directly linking it to formation of the malonylated end products.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by transient combinatorial reconstruction and LC-MS metabolite profiling.
